data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Amazon.com Stock In The Last 10 Years
data.description:
Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 10.26% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 24.06%. Currently, Amazon.com has a market capitalization of $2.55 trillion.

Buying $100 In AMZN: If an investor had bought $100 of AMZN stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $829.56 today based on a price of $238.35 for AMZN at the time of writing.

Amazon.com's Performance Over Last 10 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.

data.title: VanEck Taps Avalanche With New Crypto ETF, Targets Institutional Blockchain Adoption
data.description:
VanEck launched the VanEck Avalanche ETF (NASDAQ:VAVX) on Monday, further expanding its suite of ETFs focused on digital assets. The new fund is the first ETP listed in the U.S. to offer investors access to the price movements and staking rewards of Avalanche's native token, AVAX.

According to the company, the new ETF is designed to provide a transparent, exchange-traded way to gain access to the Avalanche blockchain ecosystem, which the company believes will play a key role in the next phase of institutional blockchain adoption.

"Avalanche's architecture is uniquely positioned to bridge the gap between traditional finance and the on-chain economy, focusing on verifiable, real-world utility," the company said.

Fee Waiver And Institutional Appeal

In connection with the listing, VanEck also announced that it is waiving all sponsor fees for VAVX on the first $500 million of assets under management or until Feb 28, whichever is earlier. The company stated that the waiver of sponsor fees aims to remove entry barriers and offer cost-efficient access to digital assets via the ETP solution.

Avalanche has been identified as a scalable blockchain platform with high-performance capabilities, allowing for the development of interconnected and customizable Layer 1 blockchains on public and private networks. The platform's consensus algorithm enables near-instant finality of transactions, which has been appealing to institutional players and corporations. VanEck pointed out that Citigroup, FIFA, and Gunzilla Games have already developed their own blockchains on the Avalanche platform.

Kyle DaCruz, director of Digital Assets Product with VanEck, explained that Avalanche's architecture is designed to bridge the gap between traditional finance and on-chain applications by bringing verifiable, real-world utility. This launch is part of a broader push to improve investor access to digital assets.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Driving The Surge In Zoom Stock?
data.description:
Zoom Communications Inc (NASDAQ:ZM) is trading higher on Monday as investors take a fresh look at the company's early bet on AI startup Anthropic, which some Baird investment analysts now believe could be worth $2 billion to $4 billion.

Can Zoom Turn Its AI Bet Into a Comeback Story?

According to Baird, Zoom's 2023 investment in Anthropic may end up returning roughly 78 times its original value, based on Anthropic's current $350 billion valuation, CNBC reported. The exact size of Zoom's investment was never disclosed, but SEC filings show the company made $51 million in "strategic investments" that quarter.

In a note on Monday, the analysts called the stake a "hidden gem," saying that while the market has been focused on Zoom's efforts to reaccelerate revenue and expand its AI offerings, the Anthropic investment could prove far more valuable.

Zoom's shares have struggled to regain momentum since the pandemic-era boom faded and workers returned to offices, making the potential multibillion‑dollar windfall from Anthropic especially significant for the company.

Technical Analysis For Zoom

Zoom is currently trading above its key moving averages, indicating bullish momentum. The stock’s position, trading 11.9% above its 20-day SMA and 18.1% above its 200-day SMA, suggests a strong upward trend.

The RSI is at 53.11, which is considered neutral, indicating that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold at this time. This level suggests that there could be room for further upward movement. MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock, suggesting a weakening momentum, so traders should watch for any shifts that could signal a change in trend strength.

Key support is at $81.50, which traders should monitor closely; a breach below this level could indicate a trend reversal. Without a defined resistance level, the stock may continue to push higher, especially if it maintains its current momentum.

The golden cross in October, when the 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA, marked a significant bullish signal for Zoom. This crossover often indicates a longer-term uptrend, reinforcing the current positive sentiment.

Over the past 12 months, Zoom has gained 15.94%, reflecting a solid performance amid market fluctuations. This longer-term trend suggests that the stock has been resilient and could continue to attract buyers if the momentum persists.

Positioned at 112.9% of its 52-week range, the stock is currently near its highs, which can be a double-edged sword. While this suggests strong bullish sentiment, it also raises the risk of a pullback if profit-taking occurs.

ZM Price Action: Zoom shares were up 11.17% at $95.36 at the time of publication on Monday. The stock is trading at a new 52-week high, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Koshiro K/Shutterstock
